title: Build a Theme Management System
description: Implement a scalable global theme system using React Context, a custom useTheme hook, three theme modes, and localStorage persistence — the pattern used in production apps from Vercel to GitHub.
requirements: Create ThemeContext with TypeScript types for preference and setter function types
Build ThemeProvider component that wraps the entire application
Implement light, dark, and system theme modes
Resolve system mode using prefers-color-scheme media query
Apply resolved theme to document root via data-theme attribute or class
Extract useTheme custom hook that throws if used outside provider
Persist theme preference to localStorage on change
Restore persisted preference on initial load
Build a theme toggle UI control accessible from the app shell
Responsive layout that works on mobile and desktop
acceptanceCriteria: Switching to dark mode updates all themed components immediately
System mode follows OS preference and updates when OS theme changes
Reloading the page restores the last selected theme preference
Components outside ThemeProvider are not part of the build — provider is at app root
useTheme returns { theme, setTheme } or equivalent typed API
No unnecessary re-renders from unstable context value objects
npm run build passes with strict TypeScript
hints: Keep resolved theme separate from preference — system resolves at runtime.
Memoize context value with useMemo so consumers do not re-render on unrelated parent updates.
Read localStorage in useState initializer: () => stored ?? 'system'.
Listen to matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)') for system mode changes.
Place ThemeProvider above routes but inside any router if navigation needs theme.

bonus.title: Production polish
bonus.description: Optional enhancements that demonstrate senior-level theme architecture.
requirements: Prevent flash of wrong theme on first paint with inline script or CSS default
Split ThemeProvider into context definition file and provider implementation
Add keyboard shortcut to cycle themes
Document theme tokens in a CSS variables file consumed by data-theme

debuggingScenarios.title: Context re-render issues
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Every component re-renders when any unrelated state changes in the provider
debuggingScenarios.cause: Context value is a new object literal on every render without useMemo
debuggingScenarios.fix: Memoize value with useMemo([theme, setTheme]); split read/write into separate contexts if needed

debuggingScenarios.title: Wrong provider placement
debuggingScenarios.symptom: useTheme throws or theme does not apply to navbar or routed pages
debuggingScenarios.cause: ThemeProvider placed inside a route or below components that need theme
debuggingScenarios.fix: Wrap entire app at root — typically in main.tsx or AppProviders above RouterProvider

debuggingScenarios.title: Theme persistence bugs
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Theme resets to default on reload or flashes wrong theme before correcting
debuggingScenarios.cause: Reading localStorage in useEffect after first render instead of initializer; writing on every render
debuggingScenarios.fix: Initialize state from localStorage synchronously; persist in useEffect only when preference changes
